items.text: A relation f from A to B associating every element of A to a unique element of B.
For f:A→B, A is the Domain and B is the Co-domain.
The set of all images of elements of A in B. Range ⊆ Co-domain.
A function that is both One-One and Onto.
Order: Matrix with 'm' rows & 'n' cols has order m × n. Trace (Tr): Sum of principal diagonal elements. Tr(A) = a₁₁ + a₂₂ + a₃₃
All elements 0 (Null), diagonal = 1 (Identity), non-diagonal = 0 (Diagonal), diagonal equal (Scalar)
Maximum order of the non-singular square sub-matrices of A.
Rank=3 if |A|≠0; Rank=2 if |A|=0 but at least one 2×2 sub-matrix ≠0; Rank=1 if all 2×2 sub-matrices=0; Rank=0 for null matrix.

items.sub: Line through a, parallel to b
Plane through 3 points a, b, c

items.latex: \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = a_1 b_1 + a_2 b_2 + a_3 b_3
\vec{a} \times \vec{b} = \begin{vmatrix} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ a_1 & a_2 & a_3 \\ b_1 & b_2 & b_3 \end{vmatrix}

items.sub: Area of Triangle (adj. sides a, b)

items.text: 180° ± θ, 360° ± θ ⇒ No change in function names

items.latex: f(x+k) = f(x) \Rightarrow f(x) \text{ is periodic with period } k \; (k>0, \text{ least})
\max(a\cos x + b\sin x + c) = c + \sqrt{a^2 + b^2}
\min(a\cos x + b\sin x + c) = c - \sqrt{a^2 + b^2}
\tan\left(\frac{\pi}{4}+A\right)=\frac{1+\tan A}{1-\tan A}=\frac{\cos A+\sin A}{\cos A-\sin A}
\cot\left(\frac{\pi}{4}+A\right)=\frac{\cot A-1}{\cot A+1}=\frac{\cos A-\sin A}{\cos A+\sin A}
\cos2A = \cos^2 A - \sin^2 A = 2\cos^2 A -1 = 1-2\sin^2 A = \frac{1-\tan^2 A}{1+\tan^2 A}

items.sub: sin²θ = sin²α, cos²θ = cos²α, tan²θ = tan²α

items.latex: \cosh 2x = \cosh^2 x + \sinh^2 x = 2\cosh^2 x -1 = 1+2\sinh^2 x = \frac{1+\tanh^2 x}{1-\tanh^2 x}
a = 2R\sin A, \; b = 2R\sin B, \; c = 2R\sin C
a^2 = b^2 + c^2 - 2bc\cos A \Rightarrow \cos A = \frac{b^2 + c^2 - a^2}{2bc}
b^2 = c^2 + a^2 - 2ac\cos B \Rightarrow \cos B = \frac{c^2 + a^2 - b^2}{2ac}
c^2 = a^2 + b^2 - 2ab\cos C \Rightarrow \cos C = \frac{a^2 + b^2 - c^2}{2ab}
\Delta = \frac{1}{2}bc\sin A = \frac{1}{2}ca\sin B = \frac{1}{2}ab\sin C = 2R^2 \sin A \sin B \sin C

items.text: If a directed line makes angles α, β, γ with positive axes of x, y and z, then cosα, cosβ, cosγ are called direction cosines (l, m, n).
d.r's of line joining A(x₁,y₁,z₁) & B(x₂,y₂,z₂) are (x₂−x₁, y₂−y₁, z₂−z₁)

items.latex: l = \frac{\pm a}{\sqrt{a^2+b^2+c^2}}, \; m = \frac{\pm b}{\sqrt{a^2+b^2+c^2}}, \; n = \frac{\pm c}{\sqrt{a^2+b^2+c^2}}
\frac{1}{2}\begin{vmatrix} x_1 & y_1 & 1 \\ x_2 & y_2 & 1 \\ x_3 & y_3 & 1 \end{vmatrix}

items.text: Area of triangle formed by 3 points = 0 ⟹ points are collinear.
x = X + h, y = Y + k ⟹ X = x − h, Y = y − k
x = Xcosθ − Ysinθ, y = Xsinθ + Ycosθ

items.latex: x = x_1 \pm r\cos\theta, \quad y = y_1 \pm r\sin\theta
\begin{vmatrix} a_1 & b_1 & c_1 \\ a_2 & b_2 & c_2 \\ a_3 & b_3 & c_3 \end{vmatrix} = 0
ax^2 + 2hxy + by^2 = 0 \text{ represents 2 lines if } h^2 \ge ab

items.sub: Area of triangle with line lx+my+n=0

items.text: f:[a,b]→R is continuous on [a,b], differentiable on (a,b), and f(a)=f(b)
f:[a,b]→R is continuous on [a,b] and differentiable on (a,b)

items.latex: f'(a) = 0 \Rightarrow x = a \text{ is a stationary (turning) point}
f'(a) = 0 \text{ and } f''(a) < 0 \Rightarrow \text{local maxima at } x=a
f'(a) = 0 \text{ and } f''(a) > 0 \Rightarrow \text{local minima at } x=a
